message:
feat(doctor): add performance check for memory and search latency

Add a new "perf" check type to the doctor command for runtime
performance monitoring.

## New Check: ms doctor --check perf

### Memory Usage (Linux only)

Reads /proc/self/statm to get resident set size (RSS):
- Warns if RSS exceeds 100 MB (configurable threshold)
- Reports actual memory usage in MB
- Gracefully skips on non-Linux platforms

### Search Latency

Benchmarks a simple FTS query to detect performance issues:
- Executes ctx.db.search_fts("test", 1)
- Warns if latency exceeds 50ms
- Reports actual latency in verbose mode

## Motivation

These checks help diagnose:
- Memory leaks from large skill indexes
- Slow search due to unoptimized FTS indexes
- Performance regression after schema migrations

## Test Updates

Updated available_checks test to include "perf" in the documented
check types list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
